title: nickname_rate_limits DashMap grows without bound — never evicted
description: relay/src/state.rs AppState::disconnect() removes a pub_id from rate_limits (broadcast rate limit) when the last connection disconnects. But it does NOT remove the pub_id from nickname_rate_limits. Every user who ever sets a nickname leaves a permanent entry in the nickname_rate_limits DashMap until the relay restarts. At a relay with 100K historical users, this is 100K Instant values (16 bytes each on x86_64) = ~1.6 MB — small but unbounded. More importantly, the code comment says 'Per-pub_id SET_NICKNAME rate limit' but the eviction is missing. The broadcast rate_limits has a matching eviction at disconnect; this is an inconsistency that a contributor would copy as a pattern without realizing one has it and the other does not.
